pattern_recognition: Matrix Traversal - The problem involves traversing a 2D matrix in a specific order (spiral). Signals include the need to manage boundaries and direction changes.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Traverse the matrix layer by layer, collecting elements in a spiral order by manually managing the boundaries.
approaches.algorithm: Start from the top-left corner, move right until the end of the row, then move down the last column, move left on the bottom row, and finally move up the first column. Repeat until all elements are collected.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use a systematic approach to track the boundaries of the spiral traversal, adjusting them after each complete loop.
approaches.algorithm: Initialize four boundaries (top, bottom, left, right). Use a loop to traverse in the order: left to right, top to bottom, right to left, and bottom to top. After each direction, adjust the respective boundary.

dry_run: Consider the input matrix: [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7, 8, 9]]. Start at (0,0), move right to (0,2) collecting [1, 2, 3]. Move down to (2,2) collecting [6, 9]. Move left to (2,0) collecting [8, 7]. Move up to (1,0) collecting [4, 5]. The final output is [1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 8, 7, 4, 5].
edge_cases: An empty matrix should return an empty list. A single element matrix should return that element. Duplicates within the matrix should be handled correctly. Large matrices should be processed without performance issues.
common_pitfalls: Common mistakes include: 1) Not updating boundaries correctly, leading to repeated elements. 2) Incorrectly handling the transition between directions, causing missed elements. 3) Failing to account for non-square matrices, resulting in index errors.
